Adult BMI cut-offs (WHO)

Source: R/data-cutoffs.R
moon_who_cutoffs.Rd

WHO adult body-mass index cut-offs that separate the four MOON adult weight states. Each entry gives the BMI value at which a person leaves one state and enters the next — i.e. the lower bound of the next state in the sequence NW → OW → OB1 → OB2.

Usage

moon_who_cutoffs

Format

A named numeric vector of length 3:

NW

25 — separates NW from OW (overweight threshold).

OW

30 — separates OW from OB1 (obese threshold).

OB1

35 — separates OB1 from OB2 (severe obesity threshold).

Source

World Health Organization. Obesity: preventing and managing the global epidemic. WHO Technical Report Series 894. Geneva: WHO; 2000.

Details

Above age 18, MOON classifies BMI directly against this vector. Below age 18 it uses moon_iotf_cutoffs instead.

See also

moon_iotf_cutoffs for the age- and sex-specific child equivalents.

On this page

Developed by Nicolai Damslund, Gudrun M W Bjørnelv, Yufei Jiang, Magnus Skov Larsen, Christina Hansen Edwards, Vidar Halsteinli, Rønnaug A Ødegaard, Line Planck Kongstad.
